title: Carnitas Tacos
description: A traditional Mexican dish of slow-cooked pork, shredded and fried until crispy, served in a taco shell with your favorite toppings.

ingredients.name: Pork shoulder, boneless
ingredients.notes: You can also use pork belly or pork jowl for a different flavor.

ingredients.name: Lard or vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: You can also use bacon drippings for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can add more onions if you like a stronger flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: You can add more garlic if you like a stronger flavor.

ingredients.name: Orange, juiced
ingredients.notes: You can use lime juice as a substitute if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: You can add more cumin if you like a stronger flavor.

ingredients.name: Taco shells
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or homemade taco shells.

ingredients.name: Shredded cheese, optional
ingredients.notes: You can use cheddar, Monterey Jack, or your favorite cheese.

ingredients.name: Salsa, optional
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or homemade salsa.

ingredients.name: Cilantro, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can use parsley as a substitute if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Lime wedges
ingredients.notes: You can use lemon wedges as a substitute if you prefer.

instructions: 1. Heat the lard or oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and cook until it's translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
4. Add the pork shoulder and cook until it's browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
5. Add the orange juice, cumin, and 1 cup of water to the pot. Cover the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce the heat to low and simmer the pork for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until it's tender and easily shreds with a fork.
7. Remove the pork from the pot and shred it with two forks. Strain the cooking liquid and discard the solids.
8. Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
9. Add the shredded pork to the skillet and cook until it's crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes.
10. Warm the taco shells according to the package instructions.
11. Assemble the tacos by spooning the carnitas onto a warmed taco shell, followed by your desired toppings.
